title: Hook injections dedupe per session: full block on first touch, one-line delta on identical repeats
context: Shipped from the roadmap. Measured in-session: identical 10-16KB grounding blocks re-injected on every edit (20+ repeats), and the availability reminder re-injected every prompt.
decision: src/core/hookcache.ts keeps a per-session {key → content-hash} map in the OS tmpdir (session_id from the hook event). First injection per file — or ANY change in the underlying records — emits the full block; an identical repeat emits a one-liner ('grounding unchanged — N decisions, M invariants still current; hunch_why to re-expand'). The UserPromptSubmit availability reminder fires once per session (a correction nudge always comes through — different content). Failure posture inverts safely: any cache error → full injection (grounded beats deduped); deny decisions never route through the cache — the gate re-checks every edit. Kill switch HUNCH_HOOK_DEDUP=0. Proven e2e: second identical edit produced the delta line.
alternatives_rejected: Suppressing repeats entirely (emit nothing): the agent loses the anchor that grounding EXISTS for the file; the one-liner keeps ids current at ~1% of the cost
State inside .hunch/: session caches are derived, per-machine noise — tmpdir with a 48h sweep keeps the repo clean
Deduping the deny path: a gate that skips re-checking is not a gate
